problems.name: Alcohol-related cirrhosis, decompensated
problems.notes: Decompensated with portal hypertension, varices, ascites. Active alcohol use (not in remission). MELD rose sharply during this admission (to ~40 at the peak of her ICU illness).

problems.name: Involuntary weight loss (~20 lb over 3 months) — under evaluation
problems.notes: Three months of unintentional weight loss preceding this admission. Differential remains open (progression of cirrhosis/anorexia, malignancy including HCC given cirrhosis + smoking + maternal breast cancer, malabsorption, depression/poor intake). Workup initiated; no etiology established at the time of the acute presentation.

problems.name: Esophageal varices with acute variceal hemorrhage — s/p attempted banding + emergent TIPS
problems.notes: Massive hematemesis shortly after admission → massive transfusion protocol, intubation for airway protection, ICU. Bedside EGD showed bleeding varices; banding attempted but failed. Emergent TIPS performed with resolution of bleeding. Ceftriaxone started for variceal-bleed/SBP prophylaxis.

problems.name: New ascites with portal hypertension
problems.notes: New ascites on admission imaging; diagnostic paracentesis with 86 PMN and no organisms on Gram stain — NEGATIVE for spontaneous bacterial peritonitis.

problems.name: Bilateral pleural effusions (hepatic hydrothorax)
problems.notes: Bilateral effusions on admission chest X-ray, consistent with hepatic hydrothorax in the setting of portal hypertension.

problems.name: Anemia and thrombocytopenia
problems.notes: Hemoglobin 8.6 (worsened by the variceal bleed) and platelets 80 K (cirrhosis/splenic sequestration).

problems.name: Shock, pressor-dependent — initially presumed septic
problems.notes: Post-TIPS hypotension requiring vasopressors, presumed septic and treated empirically with broad-spectrum antibiotics. NOTE: despite an extensive infectious workup, NO source was identified (see fevers problem and the negative culture/imaging/CSF studies).

problems.name: Persistent fevers of uncertain source
problems.notes: High fevers beginning after the TIPS, persisting despite escalating broad-spectrum antibiotics. Blood cultures NGTD, repeat paracentesis negative for SBP, CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with no abscess, lumbar puncture with normal CSF. An infectious source has not been found.

problems.name: Persistent altered mental status / unresponsiveness
problems.notes: Somnolent/unresponsive after the procedure and not improving despite weaning sedation. Differential includes hepatic encephalopathy (which TIPS can precipitate/worsen), critical illness, and other (medication-related) causes. Normal CSF; imaging without an acute structural cause.

problems.name: Acute kidney injury
problems.notes: Worsening creatinine during the ICU course, contributing to a rising MELD (peak ~40). Multifactorial (shock, hepatorenal physiology).

problems.name: Presumed drug fever and drug-associated neurotoxicity
problems.notes: After the patient was transitioned to comfort measures and antibiotics were stopped, her fevers and hypotension resolved and her mental status recovered — implicating the antibiotics (drug fever and drug-associated neurotoxicity) as the cause, a diagnosis not fully considered while the team remained anchored on occult infection. Added to the problem list only after this became apparent.
